item.title: How Trump’s Tariffs Plus Iran War May Help US Manufacturing
item.description: Ironically, America’s competitive edge has increased due to the conflict, as the price of natural gas soars for the rest of the world but falls in the US.

item.title: Markets Are Too Blasé on War’s Economic Toll, Policymakers Warn
item.description: The International Monetary Fund and World Bank are historically beacons of free trade, capitalism and financial market wisdom. But at their spring meetings, the emerging theme has a contrarian vibe: Investors are underestimating the economic damage from the Iran war.

item.title: Sheinbaum Eyes Fracking Plan in Mexico to Lessen Reliance on US Imports
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum wants to see more domestic natural gas production to ease a longstanding dependence on US supplies, including potentially using fracking techniques that she thinks can sidestep major ecological concerns.

item.title: Avis’s 200% Surge Lures New Shorts Eyeing Trip ‘Back to Reality’
item.description: A three-fold surge in the shares of car rental company Avis Budget Group in just two weeks has punished short investors betting against the stock. But the bears are not giving up; if anything, they are doubling down.

item.title: Skild AI Acquires Zebra Technologies’ Robotics Automation Business
item.description: Skild AI, a fast-rising startup that makes software to help robots learn to complete tasks, has bought the robotics automation division of Zebra Technologies Corp., marking its latest effort to broaden its reach in a hot segment.

item.title: BofA Sees 60% Jump in Commodities Trading Fueled by Oil and Gold
item.description: Bank of America Corp.’s commodities-trading revenue surged 60% in the first quarter, riding the wild market swings that pushed markets businesses at the biggest US banks to record highs.

item.title: US Treasury Nixes Cyber Intelligence Sharing Plan With Banks
item.description: Program struggled to get traction in the financial industry, people said.

item.title: Colombia Buys Dollars Ahead of $4 Billion Bond Buyback Plan
item.description: Colombia’s government is buying up dollars this week as authorities gear up for an external debt buyback, triggering losses for the nation’s currency.

item.title: Alphabet Poised for $100 Billion Windfall on SpaceX Investment
item.description: An early investment in SpaceX has positioned Alphabet Inc. for a 12-figure windfall, a new filing shows, underscoring the vast wealth likely to be created by the rocket company’s market debut.

item.title: Trump Crypto Firm Draws Scrutiny With Plan to Delay Investor Trading
item.description: World Liberty Financial Inc., the crypto project co-founded by President Donald Trump, is facing a fresh round of criticism after proposing that tokens owned by some early investors remain unavailable for trading indefinitely — or at least until Trump leaves office.

item.title: Kalshi Expands Commodities Predictions Market on War Volatility
item.description: Predictions market platform Kalshi Inc. is boosting its commodities offerings with new contracts for everything from corn and coffee to diesel and natural gas as the war in Iran attracts more traders to the betting trend.

item.title: Fed’s Beige Book Says War Drives Uncertainty for US Companies
item.description: US economic activity continued to increase at a slight-to-modest pace across most regions as the war with Iran generated a new wave of uncertainty and higher energy costs, the Federal Reserve said.

item.title: UN Awaits Go-Ahead to Move Fertilizer Through Hormuz Strait
item.description: The United Nations is ready to set up a corridor to allow fertilizer to move freely through the Strait of Hormuz and reach farmers for the planting season — but doing so hinges on a political agreement to go forward, according to a top UN official.

item.title: Oracle-Tied $38 Billion Debt Takes Months To Spread Across The Market
item.description: It was an audacious bet by JPMorgan Chase & Co. and Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group: Taking on a record-shattering $38 billion loan package backing new Oracle Corp. data center projects in Texas and Wisconsin.

item.title: SoftBank Raises $3.6 Billion While AI Push Raises Debt Costs
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. sold about $3.6 billion of junk bonds Wednesday, as aggressive investments in artificial intelligence by the Japanese conglomerate has helped fuel a surge in the firm’s funding costs.

item.title: Freedom Forever Files for Bankruptcy as Solar Industry Woes Deepen
item.description: Freedom Forever, a once thriving residential solar installer, joins a growing list of bankruptcies as distress in the US clean energy sector deepens under an unfriendly Trump administration and hopes for buyouts fade.

item.title: Goldman, Morgan Stanley Predict IPO Resilience as Revenue Climbs
item.description: Wall Street’s biggest banks kicked off 2026 with strong gains in equity capital markets revenue, even as the war in Iran weighed on IPO activity.

item.title: LA Ports’ March Imports Hold Steady Amid Tariff, War Turmoil
item.description: The stream of imported goods through Southern California’s twin ports fell about 1% last month compared to the year before, as the Iran war adds pressure and uncertainty to the global supply chain.

item.title: Italy’s Edison Replaces Most Qatar LNG Cargoes Disrupted by War
item.description: Edison SpA said it has replaced most Qatari gas supplies disrupted by the war in the Middle East, and sees no immediate risk to serving its customers in Italy.

item.title: US Probes Suspicious Oil Trades Made Before Trump Iran Pivots
item.description: The top US derivatives regulator is investigating a series of suspiciously well-timed trades in the oil futures market ahead of recent policy pivots by President Donald Trump related to the war in Iran, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Finance Chief Says Colombia Should Raise Its 3% Inflation Target
item.description: Colombian Finance Minister Germán Ávila called for nation to raise its 3% inflation target.

item.title: Google Debuts Standalone Gemini App for Apple’s MacOS
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google released a standalone Gemini app for macOS, giving owners of Apple Inc.’s Mac computers an easier way of accessing the artificial intelligence assistant.

item.title: Global Investors Boost Dollar-Hedging Ratio to Two-Year High
item.description: International investors are boosting their hedges against dollar declines as the US-Iran ceasefire erodes the haven demand that had bolstered the greenback while the fighting raged on.

item.title: Tariff Refunds Face Delays Over Slow Importer Opt-In, US Says
item.description: The Trump administration is flagging an issue that it says could delay refunds of about $166 billion in tariffs that were overturned by the US Supreme Court earlier this year.

item.title: Goldman’s Bitcoin ETF Push Signals Wall Street Taming of Crypto
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. this week became the latest major Wall Street firm to file for a Bitcoin ETF — joining Morgan Stanley, BlackRock Inc. and a growing roster of institutions racing to package cryptocurrencies for mainstream investors.

item.title: IMF Chief Warns Hasty Central-Bank Action Could Suffocate Growth
item.description: Central banks should resist the urge to hike interest rates in response to the Middle East crisis as this could damage economic output, according to International Monetary Fund Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva.

item.title: TSMC Rides Retail Frenzy to New Stock Highs as AI Boom Returns
item.description: A surge of retail investor buying has helped drive shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. to a record high as the artificial intelligence trade roars back to life.

item.title: AI Pivot Pushes Bitcoin Miners Toward a 70% Revenue Milestone
item.description: The companies that built billion-dollar businesses mining Bitcoin are on track to generate most of their revenue from artificial intelligence by the end of the year — a milestone that marks an entire industry’s pivot away from the cryptocurrency that created it.

item.title: Nagel Echoes Lagarde on Euro Zone Slipping Below ECB Baseline
item.description: The euro-zone economy has shifted beyond the European Central Bank’s base case and toward a more painful outcome due to the Iran war, Governing Council member Joachim Nagel said — echoing an earlier assessment by President Christine Lagarde.

item.title: Kerrygold Butter Maker Sees Iran War Costs Hitting Consumers
item.description: Ireland’s Ornua, the maker of Kerrygold butter, said higher costs resulting from the Middle East conflict are showing up in supply chains, in the latest warning that the war is driving food inflation.
